name: Pressure Cooker Kale/Potato Risotto
recipeIngredient: 2 medium boiling potatoes with skin, halved
1 large bunch kale or 2 small bunches kale, stems removed and leaves chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. In a pressure cooker or skillet, heat the olive oil and butter.
2. Add onions, potatoes, and kale. Sauté over medium-high heat until the kale wilts slightly and onions release their aroma.
3. (Optional: Prepare ahead to this point, cover tightly, and refrigerate until 15 to 20 minutes before serving.)
4. Add rice to the potato-kale mixture and stir briefly until the rice becomes translucent, about 1 minute.
5. Add white wine and stir until evaporated.
6. Add boiling stock, close the pressure cooker, and bring to pressure.
7. When the pressure regulator weight rocks regularly, turn the heat to medium and cook for 5 minutes.
8. Reduce pressure quickly by running cold water over the lid.
9. Stir the risotto, taste, and if necessary, cook further to the desired consistency.
10. Add 3/4 cup Parmigiano-Reggiano cheese. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
11. Garnish with remaining cheese and parsley. Serve as 6 main dishes or 12 appetizers.
